query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## US SPR — Current Level & Weekly Trajectory (EIA WCSSTUS1)

**Current level:** As of the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report (released July 1, 2026, for the week ending **June 26, 2026**), SPR crude stocks were **325,655 thousand barrels (~325.7 million barrels)** — the **lowest since 1983** and the **14th consecutive weekly draw**.

**Trajectory over the past 3–6 months:**
- **Jan–Mar 2026:** Flat-to-rising, plateauing at a peak of **~415.4 million barrels** (mid-Feb through mid-March) — the tail end of the prior refill effort.
- **Late March onward:** Draws began (week ending 3/27) and accelerated sharply.
- **Apr–Jun 2026:** Sustained releases of typically **4–10 million barrels/week**, totaling **~89.8 million barrels drawn in ~13 weeks**.

**Why (context, not forecast):** On **March 11, 2026**, President Trump authorized DOE to release **172 million barrels** as the U.S. share of a coordinated 400-million-barrel IEA release, responding to the **war with Iran and closure of the Strait of Hormuz**. DOE said delivery would take ~120 days (mid-March to ~mid-July 2026), explaining the steady heavy weekly draws through June.

Key sources: EIA WCSSTUS1 weekly series, EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, EIA "Today in Energy," RBN Energy, and Wikipedia's SPR entry — all cross-validated. Full structured data (20 weekly values + context data points) has been recorded.
